workflow: Evidence-based (CBMI does not perform processing; chain evaluates Agisoft processing report + deliverables + manifest)
global_gate_action: Sets deliverable_output_score = 0, which zeros processing_score via block-of-blocks. PROC_OUTPUT_CRS_MISMATCH fires with severity CATASTROPHIC.
verification_status_field: Separate categorical field reporting CP-related quality. VALUES: VERIFIED_RESIDUALS_PASS (CPs ≥5 AND CP_RMSE ≤target), VERIFIED_RESIDUALS_MARGINAL (CPs ≥5 AND CP_RMSE 1-2× target), VERIFIED_RESIDUALS_FAIL (CPs ≥5 AND CP_RMSE >2× target), UNVERIFIED_INSUFFICIENT_CPS (1-4 CPs), UNVERIFIED_NO_CPS (0 CPs). NEVER score-gating. Surveys can be high-quality without verification; the score reflects quality, verification_status reports independent verification result.
null_handling: processing_score = null when processing report is not uploaded (Option A locked). Per-deliverable views in v1.1 also return null when their underlying deliverable file is missing. Note: this is meaningfully different from pre_processing_score which uses Option B (report optional with graceful degradation) — because processing has almost no non-report evidence (the report IS the evidence base), Option B would produce fictional scores.
scope_note: Deep-diagnostic chain for processing-stage failures. CBMI does NOT run Agisoft — this chain evaluates the Agisoft processing report + deliverable files + processing manifest. Owns the moment-of-truth check-point residual analysis. Runtime-independent of upstream chains — uses pre_processing source artifacts and manifest (NOT pre_processing_score outputs) for cross-source comparisons. Cross-subsystem coupling deferred to future capture_score integration layer.
source_file_set: Agisoft Metashape Processing Report (required), Processing Manifest (required), Deliverable Files (required for completeness check and v1.1 per-deliverable views), Pre-Processing Handoff Data (for cross-source comparisons).
paths_supported: Agisoft Metashape v1.7.x reference; v1.6+ parser keys off section headers not page numbers. Other engines (ODM, Pix4D, RealityCapture) deferred to v2+.
known_limitations: Processing report is REQUIRED — without it, processing_score is null. The majority of third-party-processed surveys may lack the Agisoft report; for those, the future delivery layer evaluates deliverable artifacts directly. processing_score serves the report-bearing population only. Localized reconstruction collapse (#37, IM7) detection is PARTIAL in v1 — uses global proxies (tie density, reprojection). Full spatial detection of collapse zones requires v2 density-map visual analysis. The mining-most-relevant failure case (CP RMSE passes while volumes wrong because CPs do not sit on collapsed region) is partially detected only. Per-camera position outliers (#8, BA3) detection is ENSEMBLE-ONLY in v1. Per-camera detail visible only in Camera Locations ellipse map figure; v2 visual analysis required for outlier detection. Software version known-buggy list (#33, DO8) is v2 deliverable — v1 carries the indicator as advisory only with zero scoring weight. CP-GCP statistical independence check (from GCP#2) deferred to v2 — needs statistical analysis beyond report fields. High altitude variance (from Drone FLG_011) deferred to v2 — needs visual overlap-map analysis. Parser must key off section headers (Survey Data / Camera Calibration / Camera Locations / Ground Control Points / Digital Elevation Model / Processing Parameters) NOT page numbers, since Agisoft changes layout between versions. Every evidence cite has been validated against the v1.7.6 reference reports. Cross-source comparison rows (CV4 role consistency, CV5 GCP typo, DO3 internal transform) are dual-dependency — they require both Agisoft report and pre_processing handoff data. When manifest or pre_processing data is incomplete, these indicators degrade to N/A. BA4 (optimization completeness) threshold softened per v1.0 pressure-test: missing b1/b2 → 90 (was 70). Rationale: skipping b1/b2 is a minor operator preference, not a major quality differentiator.
